title: God of War
evidence_note: Widely available on modern platforms, but source code and deeper technical materials are not publicly available.
rarity_reason: Still available on modern platforms and easy to access physically and digitally.

playable_access.reason: The game is available on modern platforms, including PC, making it relatively easy for players to access legally.

platform_dependency.reason: The game is not locked only to original PlayStation 4 hardware because a PC version exists.

documentation.reason: There is strong public discussion, reviews, guides, interviews, and analysis around the game, although deeper production archives are limited.

technical_preservation.reason: The game is playable and patched, but source code, internal tools, and full development materials are not publicly available.

community_preservation.reason: The game has an active player base, continued discussion, analysis, and long-term interest.

cultural_value.reason: The game had a major impact on cinematic action-adventure design, character-driven storytelling, and the direction of the God of War franchise.

rarity_score.reason: The game remains widely available compared with older or delisted titles.

title: The Simpsons: Hit & Run
evidence_note: No modern digital release, but strong fan interest and community preservation keep it visible.
rarity_reason: No current official digital release, older platform dependency, and strong collector demand make access harder.

playable_access.reason: The game is not currently available through modern official digital storefronts, so legal access depends mainly on older physical copies.

platform_dependency.reason: The game is tied to older platforms such as PlayStation 2, Xbox, GameCube, and original PC releases.

documentation.reason: There is decent public information, guides, fan discussion, and retrospective coverage, but it is not as deeply documented as some historically preserved titles.

technical_preservation.reason: The game has strong fan interest, but official source code, tools, and full technical materials are not publicly available.

community_preservation.reason: The game has a strong fan community, nostalgia value, modding interest, and continued online discussion.

cultural_value.reason: The game is remembered as one of the most beloved licensed games of its generation and remains culturally visible through fan demand.

rarity_score.reason: The lack of a modern official release means physical copies remain the main legal route to ownership, increasing collector pressure.

title: P.T.
evidence_note: Delisted from the PlayStation Store, difficult to legally access, but heavily documented and culturally significant.
rarity_reason: Delisted from official storefronts and only accessible through specific existing PS4 installs.

playable_access.reason: The game was delisted and is no longer normally available through official storefronts, making legal access extremely limited.

platform_dependency.reason: Access depends heavily on specific PlayStation 4 systems or accounts where the game was already installed.

documentation.reason: Although access is poor, the game is heavily documented through videos, articles, analysis, fan discussion, and horror game retrospectives.

technical_preservation.reason: The game has no public source code or official technical preservation route, and its delisted status creates additional preservation risk.

community_preservation.reason: The game remains highly discussed, analysed, recreated, and remembered by horror fans and game design communities.

cultural_value.reason: The game became one of the most famous horror demos ever released due to its connection to Silent Hills, its design impact, and its unusual delisting history.

rarity_score.reason: Because it is delisted and cannot be newly downloaded normally, access is extremely rare compared with most digital games.

title: Silent Hill 2
evidence_note: Highly influential survival horror game with strong documentation and cultural importance, but original versions remain important to preserve.
rarity_reason: Original versions are increasingly valued by collectors, and later versions do not fully replace the preservation importance of the original release.

playable_access.reason: The game has had multiple releases, but original versions remain the most historically important and are not always easy to access legally.

platform_dependency.reason: Original access is tied to older platforms such as PlayStation 2, Xbox, and early PC versions.

documentation.reason: Silent Hill 2 is heavily documented through criticism, retrospectives, fan analysis, interviews, and horror game studies.

technical_preservation.reason: Community work has helped preserve and improve some versions, but official source code and production materials are not publicly available.

community_preservation.reason: The game has a strong long-term fan community, modding interest, and critical discussion.

cultural_value.reason: Silent Hill 2 is widely regarded as one of the most important psychological horror games ever made.

rarity_score.reason: Collector demand for original versions is strong, especially because the original experience has preservation value beyond later releases.

title: Metal Gear Solid 4: Guns of the Patriots
evidence_note: Major entry in the Metal Gear series that remains strongly tied to PlayStation 3 hardware.
rarity_reason: Its platform dependency makes it harder to access compared with many other major games from the same era.

playable_access.reason: The game is playable on original PlayStation 3 hardware, but access is limited compared with games available on modern platforms.

platform_dependency.reason: The game remains closely associated with PlayStation 3 architecture and hardware access.

documentation.reason: There is strong public coverage, guides, reviews, interviews, and franchise analysis.

technical_preservation.reason: Technical preservation is limited because source code, development tools, and internal materials are not publicly available.

community_preservation.reason: The Metal Gear community remains active, and the game continues to be discussed within franchise history.

cultural_value.reason: The game is culturally important as a major narrative and technical entry in one of gaming's most recognised stealth franchises.

rarity_score.reason: The game is not extremely rare, but its hardware dependency increases collector and preservation relevance.

title: Driver: San Francisco
evidence_note: Distinctive driving game with unique mechanics and limited modern availability.
rarity_reason: Delisting and lack of easy modern access have increased its collector and preservation interest.

playable_access.reason: The game is difficult to access through modern official digital channels, making older copies more important.

platform_dependency.reason: Access mainly depends on seventh-generation consoles or older PC availability.

documentation.reason: There are reviews, guides, and fan discussions, but deeper development documentation is limited.

technical_preservation.reason: There is limited public technical preservation information, source access, or official tool documentation.

community_preservation.reason: The game has maintained a cult reputation due to its unusual shift mechanic and delisting status.

cultural_value.reason: The game is notable for its unusual design concept and its place in arcade driving game history.

rarity_score.reason: Limited modern access and collector interest increase its rarity concern.

title: Spec Ops: The Line
evidence_note: Important narrative shooter with strong critical discussion and preservation concern due to delisting.
rarity_reason: Delisting increases reliance on existing physical and previously purchased digital copies.

playable_access.reason: The game became harder to access after delisting from digital storefronts.

platform_dependency.reason: Physical access remains possible on older platforms, but modern legal digital access is weakened.

documentation.reason: The game is well documented through criticism, essays, retrospectives, and discussion of its narrative themes.

technical_preservation.reason: Technical preservation is limited because source code and internal materials are not publicly available.

community_preservation.reason: The game remains discussed because of its unusual treatment of violence, player agency, and military shooter conventions.

cultural_value.reason: It is culturally significant because it challenged mainstream military shooter design and storytelling expectations.

rarity_score.reason: Delisting creates scarcity pressure because new legal digital purchases are restricted.

title: OutRun 2006: Coast 2 Coast
evidence_note: Important arcade racing title affected by licensing and modern availability issues.
rarity_reason: Licensed content and lack of modern digital availability make original copies more important to collectors.

playable_access.reason: Modern official access is limited, increasing reliance on older physical releases.

platform_dependency.reason: The game depends on older platforms and legacy PC compatibility.

documentation.reason: The game has reviews, fan discussion, and arcade racing coverage, but limited deep preservation documentation.

technical_preservation.reason: Public technical preservation information is limited, and licensing issues complicate long-term access.

community_preservation.reason: Arcade racing fans continue to discuss and value the game.

cultural_value.reason: The game is culturally valuable as a strong later entry in Sega's arcade racing legacy.

rarity_score.reason: Licensing and limited modern access increase collector interest and preservation concern.

title: Marvel vs. Capcom 2
platform: Arcade / Dreamcast / PlayStation 2 / Xbox / Xbox 360 / PlayStation 3
evidence_note: Highly influential fighting game with strong community preservation but complicated availability history.
rarity_reason: Original releases and older digital versions have strong collector and fighting game community demand.

playable_access.reason: The game has existed across multiple platforms, but legal modern access has not always been stable.

platform_dependency.reason: Important versions are tied to arcade hardware and older home platforms.

documentation.reason: The game is heavily documented through competitive play, guides, videos, match archives, and community history.

technical_preservation.reason: Community knowledge is strong, but official source code and internal development materials are not publicly available.

community_preservation.reason: The fighting game community has kept the game alive through tournaments, match footage, discussion, and long-term competitive interest.

cultural_value.reason: It is one of the most important crossover fighting games and has major significance in competitive fighting game history.

rarity_score.reason: Demand for original and older versions remains high due to collector interest and competitive legacy.

title: Pokémon HeartGold and SoulSilver
evidence_note: Highly valued Nintendo DS remakes with strong collector interest and long-term cultural importance.
rarity_reason: Physical copies and complete boxed versions are strongly desired by collectors, especially versions with Pokéwalker accessories.

playable_access.reason: The games are playable on Nintendo DS and compatible hardware, but they are not broadly available through modern official digital routes.

platform_dependency.reason: Access depends on Nintendo DS-era hardware and cartridges.

documentation.reason: The games are heavily documented through guides, wikis, walkthroughs, fan communities, and Pokémon resources.

technical_preservation.reason: Public technical and community knowledge is strong, but official source code and development tools are not available.

community_preservation.reason: The Pokémon community is extremely active and has preserved large amounts of information, strategy, and historical discussion.

cultural_value.reason: The games are culturally important as beloved remakes of major Pokémon titles and are widely viewed as among the strongest entries in the series.

rarity_score.reason: Collector demand is high, especially for complete versions and accessory bundles.
